reasoning:

For short-duration stock forecasts (1 week to 1 month), movements follow an approximate random walk. While the general equity market has a slight positive drift, yfinance’s 60-day statistical trend analysis indicates a slightly negative linear trend for STE over the past two months (slope = -0.075 per day), and standard mean reversion from the recent bounce off 246 to 254.7 suggests slight downside pressure. Currently, STE is sitting slightly above its recent short-term moving average.

For the first resolution date (2026-03-08, using the March 6 close), the random walk with a modest short-term negative trend yields a roughly 49% probability of closing higher than 252.35. We set the first probability at 0.49.

For the second resolution date (2026-03-31), over a four-week period, the general positive equity drift mostly counteracts the short-term negative technical trend, pulling the expected probability to an essentially balanced 50/50 prediction. A one-month horizon without major catalysts like earnings results in high uncertainty symmetrically distributed around the current price. Thus, we have set the second probability at 0.50, reflecting increased uncertainty closer to 0.5 as required for more distant forecasting horizons.
